posts.hook: Children are getting myopia 2 years earlier than they did in the 90s.
posts.caption:
Kids are going nearsighted younger and faster than ever.

- Average onset of myopia: 2 years earlier than 1990
- Children spending 7+ hours/day on screens
- Less than 1 hour of outdoor time per day for most school children

Outdoor light is protective. Screen time is a risk factor.

Help kids understand their eye health early.

Free tools at axeo.vision

#KidsEyeHealth #ChildMyopia #ScreenTime #Axeo #AxeoVision #ParentingTips #EyeHealth

posts.hook: The convergence exercise — the one your optometrist actually prescribes.
posts.caption:
Convergence insufficiency affects 1 in 10 people.

Symptoms: headaches, double vision, losing your place when reading.

The fix: Pencil Push-Up exercise (optometrists actually prescribe this).

How to do it:
1. Hold a pencil at arm's length
2. Focus on the tip
3. Slowly bring it toward your nose
4. Stop when you see double
5. Push back out. Repeat 20x.

Guided version free on Axeo Premium.

axeo.vision

#Convergence #EyeExercise #Axeo #AxeoVision #PencilPushUp #EyeHealth #Optometrist

posts.hook: The pencil push-up — the exercise optometrists actually prescribe.
posts.caption:
This is a real optometrist-prescribed exercise for convergence insufficiency.

Pencil Push-Up:
1. Pencil at arm's length
2. Focus on tip
3. Bring toward nose slowly
4. Stop at double vision
5. Push back. 20 reps.

Affects 1 in 10 people. Most don't know they have it.

posts.hook: Convergence insufficiency: the vision problem no one talks about.
posts.caption:
Convergence insufficiency affects 1 in 10 people.

Most people with it don't know.

Symptoms:
- Headaches when reading
- Words moving on the page
- Eye fatigue after 20+ minutes of near work
- Occasional double vision

Often misdiagnosed as dyslexia, ADHD, or just 'not a reader'.

Treatment: vision therapy (guided exercises). No surgery. No glasses required.

Free awareness test + exercises at axeo.vision

#ConvergenceInsufficiency #VisionTherapy #Axeo #EyeHealth #LearningDifferences
posts.visual_notes: Educational explainer. Show reading scenario with simulated convergence issues. Medical but relatable. Personal story element possible.

posts.hook: Convergence insufficiency: affects 1 in 10 people, often misdiagnosed as dyslexia or ADHD.
posts.caption:
Convergence insufficiency affects ~10% of people.

Symptoms:
- Headaches reading or on screens
- Words that 'move' on the page
- Eye fatigue in under an hour

Mostly treated with vision therapy (exercises). Often mistaken for learning disabilities.

Awareness matters. axeo.vision
